memoryArtifacts.headline: Essex England 1900 era
memoryArtifacts.description:
The heavy line from Harwich ends at the southwest "corner" Which was the home of Edward Marvin.
"Spinels" adjoined it on the west. Spinels, Ramsey and Great Bentley are underlined.  "Sturwood" and East Hall" are near the Harwich Branch RR on the south bank of the Stour.

popularStory.headline: Edward Marvin 1550-1615 pg 22-23 of Descendants of Reinold and Matthew Marvin
popularStory.description:
https://archive.org/stream/descendantsrein00unkngoog#page/n31/mode/1up

22   English Ancestry of

Edward2 L9C6-MZJ son of Rynalde1 9HW4-1JW and Johan Marvin LHN6-1WT, was born in Ramsey 51°55'44.29" …

burialPlace.address: Saint Mary the Virgin Churchyard, Great Bentley, Essex, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
END OF LINE ANCESTOR: Margaret (—?—) Marven/Marvin/Mervyn

John Insley Coddington, Fellow of the American Society Genealogists, “Notes on the Ancestry of Reinold and Matthew Marvin,” The American Genealogist (18 [1941]: 1-13); online starting at:

https://www.americanancestors.org/DB283/i/11852/1/0

===

Edward and Margaret Marvin had 11 children

There is no evidence I am aware of that an Margaret Gillyat married Edward (L9C6-MZJ), rather, IF there is a Gillyat(or Pakeman), it would be the Margaret (MDS3-8BX) who mrd his Brother John (KLLZ-1CZ). 


https://archive.org/stream/englishancestryo00marv#page/116/mode/1up

John (KLLZ-1CZ) (Edwards brother) was twice married : first, to Anne (LZ2S-X3S), who died in
Little Oakley and was buried* there 11 September, 1584,
by whom he had a daughter. He married as his second
wife Margaret (MDS3-8BX), evidently in the autumn or winter of
1584/5, his Will being dated 5 March 1584/5, and his burial
is registered at St. Mary's three days later, but no record of
his marriage appears there. The allusion to Alice Gyllyat,
his "kynsewoman," and her " syster Ann Pakeman," who was
very likely the wife of Lawrence, may suggest a clue to the
* Parish Register of St. Mary's Church, Little Oakley.

Reinold and Matthew Marvin 117

maiden name of ONE of these wives, but no attempt has been
made to trace their connection.
Aside from his residence John owned several other houses
and lands, for he names four of his tenants. One of these
estates (not the homestead) he gives conditionally to his
widow during her life, and her issue, should there be a
posthumous child ; if she die childless, all is to revert to his
daughter by his first wife, who was
61 Anne b. , but evidently not of age when her father died.
Whether there was another child has not been learned.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Margaret was born about 1555, in England. She married Edward Marvin about 1580, in England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. She died before 28 May 1633, in Essex, England, and was buried in Great Bentley, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret(은)는 대략 1555년 영국에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1580년 영국에서 Edward Marvin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1633년 5월 28일 이전 영국 에세스에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 벤틀리에 묻혔다.

У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла перед 28 май 1633 г. в местности эссекс, Англия и была похоронена в местности грит бентли, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
